title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
5 Reasons SISO Is the Smarter Strategy for Long-Term Investors
Long-term investors aim to create a corpus for their long-term financial goals with disciplined investing and compounded returns. If they choose market-linked avenues, there is the risk of volatility involved. They plan to time the financial market when investing as well as when redeeming. Timing the financial markets might prove challenging since such markets are unpredictable. As such, long-term investors need a systematic approach to their investments and redemptions.
This is where SISO can prove helpful. SISO, or Systematic In, Systematic Out, offers a disciplined way to deal with the uncertainty of financial markets. By investing in a structured manner, you can save affordably and in a disciplined manner for the long term. On the other hand, systematic withdrawals can create a source of regular income for your financial goals.
What is SISO in Simple Terms?
SISO or Systematic In, Systematic Out is a systematic investment and redemption strategy offered by Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance. It allows policyholders to pay premiums monthly when buying a traditional savings-oriented life insurance plan or ULIPs (Unit-Linked Insurance Plans). This creates a habit of disciplined investing which might also prove pocket-friendly. Moreover, in ULIPs, systematic investments allow policyholders to benefit from rupee-cost averaging instead of finding the right time to enter the financial markets.
The strategy also provides policyholders with systematic and periodic payouts which can supplement their income and help them fulfil their financial goals. In ULIPs, systematic withdrawals also give the benefit of rupee-cost averaging and allows the remaining fund to grow over time.
Reasons Why SISO Is Smarter for Long-Term Investors
Some of the reasons that make SISO a good choice for long-term investors are as follows -
Reason 1: Minimises the Risk of Timing the Financial Markets
With SISO, you don’t need to wait for the right time to invest. By contributing regularly instead of in a single lump sum, investments are spread across different market phases. This consistency balances out market volatility and gives the benefit of rupee-cost averaging, making long-term growth more stable and predictable.
Reason 2: Leverages Rupee Cost Averaging
When investing in ULIPs, SISO helps you get the benefit of rupee-cost averaging. By investing a fixed amount regularly, the NAV (Net Asset Value) is averaged out over time. When the financial markets are high, fewer units are bought, and when they fall, more units are bought. This helps reduce the impact of short-term market swings and supports consistent growth in the long run.
Reason 3: Provides Predictable Income During Exit
Instead of withdrawing everything at once, SISO allows systematic withdrawals at regular intervals. This creates a steady cash flow, helping retirees or long-term investors enjoy regular income. Moreover, you get predictable income payouts from your policy. If you have ULIPs, the systematic payouts help in meeting immediate financial needs while the remaining fund value enjoys continued growth potential. This makes the exit phase more controlled and predictable.
For example, let’s say at 25 years, you start investing ₹20,000 every month in SISO with the goal of buying your own house by the age of 35. Over 10 years, your systematic contributions might have built a good corpus. When the time comes, you can start systematic withdrawals. You can withdraw a fixed amount every month to repay the home loan. This can help you avoid any financial pressure and let the remaining investment grow for your future needs.
Reason 4: Brings Discipline to Investing
Once you choose SISO and set up an auto-debit mandate on your bank account for premium payments, the fixed amount is automatically debited from the bank account every month. This prevents procrastination and ensures disciplined investing. Moreover, it can avoid investment decisions being driven by emotions during ups and downs of the financial market. This promotes consistent and disciplined savings and corpus creation over time.
Reason 5: Flexibility for Different Life Goals
With regular payouts received from SISO, you can achieve different life goals like buying a house or car, or funding your child’s education. You can even use it for retirement planning. You can also change the frequency of withdrawals as per your changing needs.
Who Should Use SISO?
SISO is suitable for investors who want to stay invested for the long term and prefer a disciplined approach to both investing and withdrawing money. It suits people who want to steadily build wealth over the years without worrying about timing the market. It is also useful for those who need a predictable income stream to fulfil their financial goals.
By combining systematic contributions with systematic withdrawals, SISO can be a smart choice for individuals who value both discipline and flexibility in their financial journey.
Conclusion
With SISO, investing becomes simple because it promotes discipline in how you invest. Moreover, with market-linked plans, SISO can reduce the stress of timing the financial markets with rupee-cost averaging.
You can create a corpus for meeting your long-term financial goals and also get a steady source of income. The added life insurance coverage can give you and your family financial security too. Whether your goal is to buy a house, fund your child’s education, or plan for retirement, SISO can prove useful. Explore the range of Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance plans that come with the SISO option and take the first step towards building a secure financial future for yourself and your loved ones.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER.
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
5 Reasons SISO Is the Smarter Strategy for Long-Term Investors
Long-term investors aim to create a corpus for their long-term financial goals with disciplined investing and compounded returns. If they choose market-linked avenues, there is the risk of volatility involved. They plan to time the financial market when investing as well as when redeeming. Timing the financial markets might prove challenging since such markets are unpredictable. As such, long-term investors need a systematic approach to their investments and redemptions.
This is where SISO can prove helpful. SISO, or Systematic In, Systematic Out, offers a disciplined way to deal with the uncertainty of financial markets. By investing in a structured manner, you can save affordably and in a disciplined manner for the long term. On the other hand, systematic withdrawals can create a source of regular income for your financial goals.
What is SISO in Simple Terms?
SISO, or Systematic In, Systematic Out, is a systematic investment and redemption feature offered by Bajaj Life Insurance on select eligible products. It allows policyholders of those specific plans to pay premiums monthly, helping build a habit of disciplined contributions that may also feel more manageable. In eligible ULIP plans, the systematic investment component can additionally help policyholders benefit from rupee-cost averaging rather than trying to time market entry.
The strategy also provides policyholders with systematic and periodic payouts which can supplement their income and help them fulfil their financial goals. In ULIPs, systematic withdrawals also give the benefit of rupee-cost averaging and allows the remaining corpus to grow over time.
Reasons Why SISO Is Smarter for Long-Term Investors Some of the reasons that make SISO a good choice for long-term investors are as follows - Reason 1: Minimises the Risk of Timing the Financial Markets
With SISO for ULIPs, you don’t need to wait for the right time to invest. By contributing regularly , investments are spread across different market phases. This consistency balances out market volatility and gives the benefit of rupee-cost averaging, making long-term growth more stable and predictable.
Reason 2: Leverages Rupee Cost Averaging
When investing in ULIPs, SISO helps you get the benefit of rupee-cost averaging. By investing a fixed amount regularly, the NAV (Net Asset Value) is averaged out over time. When the financial markets are high, fewer units are bought, and when they fall, more units are bought. This helps reduce the impact of short-term market swings and supports consistent growth in the long run.
Reason 3: Provides Predictable Income During Exit
Instead of withdrawing everything at once, SISO allows systematic withdrawals at regular intervals. This creates a steady cash flow, helping retirees or long-term investors enjoy regular income. Moreover, you get predictable income payouts from your policy. If you have ULIPs, the systematic
payouts help in meeting immediate financial needs while the remaining fund value enjoys continued growth potential. This makes the exit phase more controlled and predictable.
For example, let’s say at 25 years, you start investing ₹20,000 every month in SISO with the goal of buying your own house by the age of 35. Over 10 years, your systematic contributions might have built a good corpus. When the time comes, you can start systematic withdrawals. You can withdraw a fixed amount every month to repay the home loan. This can help you avoid any financial pressure and let the remaining investment grow for your future needs.
Reason 4: Brings Discipline to Investing
Once you choose SISO and set up an auto-debit mandate on your bank account for premium payments, the fixed amount is automatically debited from the bank account every month. This prevents procrastination and ensures disciplined investing. Moreover, it can avoid investment decisions being driven by emotions during ups and downs of the financial market. This promotes consistent and disciplined savings and corpus creation over time.
Reason 5: Flexibility for Different Life Goals With regular payouts received from SISO, you can achieve different life goals like buying a house or car, or funding your child’s education. You can even use it for retirement planning. You may also have the option to modify the withdrawal frequency, depending on the features available under the specific plan. Who Should Use SISO?
SISO is suitable for investors who want to stay invested for the long term and prefer a disciplined approach to both investing and withdrawing money. It suits people who want to steadily build wealth over the years without worrying about timing the market if they are choosing ULIPs. It is also useful for those who need a predictable income stream to fulfil their financial goals.
By combining systematic contributions with systematic withdrawals, SISO can be a smart choice for individuals who value both discipline and flexibility in their financial journey.
Conclusion
With SISO, investing becomes simple because it promotes discipline in how you invest. Moreover, with market-linked plans, SISO can reduce the stress of timing the markets through rupee-cost averaging. You can build a corpus for long-term goals and generate a steady income stream. The life insurance cover provided by those plans gives your family financial security. Whether your goal is to buy a house, fund your child’s education, or plan for retirement, SISO (available on select Bajaj Life Insurance plans) can be a useful feature to support those objectives.
